triageFlags.title: Applicant Age Below 21
triageFlags.description: The applicant's age is below the minimum threshold for standard underwriting policies.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider additional verification of financial stability and maturity.
triageFlags.reasoning: Underwriting policies typically require applicants to be at least 21 years old to ensure financial maturity and stability. Exceptions may require additional documentation or guarantees.

triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Threshold
triageFlags.description: The applicant's DTI ratio exceeds the maximum allowable threshold for their credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Request additional documentation to verify affordability or consider adjusting the loan terms.
triageFlags.reasoning: The DTI ratio is a critical measure of affordability. Exceeding the threshold suggests potential financial strain, necessitating further review or adjustments to the loan structure.

content.reasoning: The applicant's age and DTI ratio are notable concerns, but the credit score and other financial metrics are within acceptable ranges. Additional scrutiny on affordability is recommended.
